items.title: Nude swimmers take chilly plunge in Dark Mofo solstice swim

children.content: Dark Mofo's Nude Solstice Swim marks the festival end of Tasmania's fortnight-long winter event.

gallery.altText: a person wearing a red swimming cap with the words "Asses to asses" printed on it

thumbnail.altText: a person wearing a red swimming cap with the words "Asses to asses" printed on it

items.title: Mona unveils $100m underground library packed with rare literary treasures

children.content: From Shakespeare's First Folio to neon-lit shelves to a curvy kids' section, Mona's new library, Phrontisterion,
is anything but ordinary.

image.altText: Book on display with hand reaching for a dial device.

items.title: Bird flu could be catastrophic for nation's vulnerable species, experts warn

children.content: The black swan, the Tasmanian devil and Australian sea lions are among the species at risk if a deadly strain of avian influenza takes hold in Australia, experts are warning.

image.altText: Tasmanian Devils will be released into a 1000 acre sanctuary to interact with other native mainland animals.

items.title: Victoria stopped native forest logging, only to subsidise it in Tasmania

children.content: Four Corners follows the logs, and the money, to uncover how efforts to end logging have shifted the timber trade across state borders.

gallery.altText: Two yellow excavators with claw attachments sit next to piles of logs. The end of a forest can be seen in the background.

thumbnail.altText: Two yellow excavators with claw attachments sit next to piles of logs. The end of a forest can be seen in the background.

items.title: Albanese to meet with state leaders to talk fuel security

children.content: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese will convene a virtual meeting of the national cabinet to discuss fuel security.  Follow live.

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: Gambling reform consultation slammed as 'charade' by health experts

children.content: The government is preparing to push ahead with a watered-down version of recommendations from the landmark Murphy inquiry, which called for a comprehensive ban on gambling advertising.

image.altText: Football players play in front of a sportsbet banner.

items.title: A major change to super payments will start next week

children.content: Many businesses support the payday superannuation changes, but some are concerned they will not be able to meet the obligations.

articles.title: Auction clearance rates fall to lowest level in six years

children.content: Not since the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic have sellers seen such disappointing results at auction, with Cotality predicting "a further loss of momentum" in the housing market.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit gesticulates at an auction with a house behind

articles.title: Think writing off something on tax makes it free? It doesn't

children.content: Advertisers tout EOFY sales as a prime time for big buys, and hint at the promise of a generous tax refund, but that's not quite how it works.

image.altText: A collage of a series of end-of-financial-year sales tickets.

gallery.altText: Woman with blonde hair and blue shirt sits at desk looking at camera

thumbnail.altText: Woman with blonde hair and blue shirt sits at desk looking at camera

items.title: Concerns over AI and data centres a dangerous force for Labor

children.content: While the government is trying to assure people there is nothing to fear from AI, many think technology is moving too quickly for authorities to keep up.

image.altText: A man in a vampire outfit lies on the steps of an office building with protest signs against data centres behind him.

items.title: Fijians fear cartels will come looking for cocaine bricks washed up on beach

children.content: Twenty-seven bricks of cocaine have washed up around Fiji's remote Komo Island in recent weeks. Residents are worried about what the find will bring.

image.altText: a Fijian man on a beach looking out to sea

items.title: AI helped treat this Australian dog's cancer, but it faces hurdles for humans

children.content: Rosie the dog had her tumours treated by an mRNA vaccine developed with the help of artificial intelligence. Her owner had decided to explore treatment options using AI chatbots.

items.title: Backlash as council compares rate rise to bread and cheese

children.content: Residents are frustrated as councils pass increasing costs to ratepayers already struggling with the cost-of-living crisis.

image.altText: A woman holding a dog, standing in front of a garden at the front of her home.

items.title: The personalised, predatory design driving Australians to gamble online

children.content: Betting ads on TV have been front of mind in Canberra lately, but experts say we're overlooking the real firepower behind Australia's gambling industry: cutting-edge, hyper-personalised, online tech.

image.altText: A photo over the shoulder of a woman as she looks at a gambling app on her phone.

items.title: We sent six filmmakers to six countries to make a movie. How did they go?

children.content: From rug making in Morocco to small-town struggles in China, catch up on the films from the third week of the new season of Race Around The World.

gallery.altText: A woman drives a gulf buggy over a dusty plain.
A Chinese man stands in front of tall trees.
A man stands in a busy train walkway.

thumbnail.altText: A woman drives a gulf buggy over a dusty plain.
A Chinese man stands in front of tall trees.
A man stands in a busy train walkway.

items.title: Meet the artist documenting Australia's lesbian community

children.content: When Stacey Bennett came out at 14, she felt alone. Now the Geelong-based artist has painted 100 lesbians to ensure future generations know they are part of a rich and diverse community.

image.altText: A woman with curly blonde hair sitting in front of a wall of portraits of women she has painted.

items.title: History quiz: Where was Ned Kelly finally captured?

children.content: Can you answer our questions about Anna Wintour, Australian tennis royalty and the doughnuts of the past? Test yourself.

image.altText: A large statue of Ned Kelly in his armour holding a gun

items.title: Question mark over duck hunt zone's future

children.content: Duck hunters and wildlife rescuers question whether Geelong's hunting zone will be axed due to the influx of new residents so close to shooting.

gallery.altText: Man in camoflague wades through lake with suburban houses in the background

thumbnail.altText: Man in camoflague wades through lake with suburban houses in the background

items.title: ASX set to slip as fragile Iran war ceasefire keeps markets on edge

children.content: While Wall St was closed for a public holiday on Friday, global markets remain on edge as US-Iran peace talks falter. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A stock trader looks down at a tablet as he works on the floor of a busy stock exchange while surrounded by screens.

items.title: Auction clearance rates fall to lowest level in six years

children.content: Not since the early days of the COVID-19 pandemic have sellers seen such disappointing results at auction, with Cotality predicting "a further loss of momentum" in the housing market.

image.altText: A man wearing a suit gesticulates at an auction with a house behind

items.title: France on 'red alert' as temps hit 40C in Europe heatwave

children.content: Half of France is set to be placed on "red alert" as temperatures climb beyond 40 degrees Celsius across mainland Europe.

image.altText: People swim in a canal while many gather along the banks

gallery.altText: A woman holds a hand fan over her head in front of the Eiffel Tower.

thumbnail.altText: A woman holds a hand fan over her head in front of the Eiffel Tower.

items.title: Serena Williams to make Wimbledon singles comeback

children.content: Serena Williams will make a stunning return to singles at this year's Wimbledon after accepting the final wildcard berth by the All England Club.

image.altText: Serena Williams with thw 2016 Wimbledon singles trophy.

items.title: Delays to alcohol delivery restrictions worry SA anti-violence advocates

children.content: Family violence prevention advocates say the SA government has yet to progress alcohol delivery legislation as recommended by last year's Royal Commission into Domestic, Family and Sexual Violence.

image.altText: a close-up of someone using an iphone to purchase alcohol online

items.title: Queensland charity closes 'life-changing' NDIS employment support program

children.content: Wesley Mission Queensland says the changing NDIS environment has made long-term sustainability a significant challenge for its employment service.

gallery.altText: A young man in a green shirt putting fresh vegetables in a box.

thumbnail.altText: A young man in a green shirt putting fresh vegetables in a box.

avatar.alt: A woman wearing a jacket sits in a park.

items.title: Stokes returns to England captaincy for third Test against New Zealand

children.content: Captain Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have returned to England's squad for the third Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge following the conclusion of a disciplinary hearing.

image.altText: Ben Stokes in his cricketing gear and a cap scratching his head.

items.title: Australia posts seven-wicket T20I win over Bangladesh as Johnson stars with ball

children.content: Spencer Johnson proves to be a menace with the ball for Australia, as the tourists complete a T20I series clean sweep against Bangladesh.

image.altText: Spencer Johnson is congratulated by an Australian trammate after taking a wicket against Bangladesh.

items.title: Colleen Harkin quits Liberal Party after praising One Nation

children.content: Liberal Party member and think tank education director Colleen Harkin has quit the party a week after attending a One Nation fundraiser in Melbourne.

image.altText: Colleen Harkin wears a pink blazer

items.title: Dry winter conditions forecast for Queensland before rain returns

children.content: After a week of unseasonal rainfall, much of the state will return to typical winter conditions from Tuesday, with drier, cooler mornings and sunny afternoons.

image.altText: couple on ebikes looking towards ocean horizon

items.title: Microalgae genes could be key to reversing kelp's decline

children.content: Scientists are investigating whether gene editing could become a tool for helping kelp adapt to the increasing challenges posed by climate change.
